content.text: Local openssl is available, so I'll generate a fresh CA and a server certificate for demo.example.com locally, then install both into the cluster.

input.command:
set -e
WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$WORKDIR"

# New CA
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout ca.key -out ca.crt -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=Test CA" -addext "basicConstraints=critical,CA:TRUE" -addext "keyUsage=critical,keyCertSign,cRLSign" 2>/dev/null

# Server key + CSR for demo.example.com
openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout tls.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=demo.example.com" 2>/dev/null

cat > ext.cnf <<EOF
subjectAltName=DNS:demo.example.com
extendedKeyUsage=serverAuth
EOF

openssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out tls.crt -days 825 -extfile ext.cnf 2>/dev/null

echo "---verify---"
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt tls.crt
openssl x509 -in tls.crt -noout -subject -dates -ext subjectAltName
ls -la
echo "$WORKDIR" > /tmp/tls_workdir.txt

content.text: Valid cert chain generated. Now install the TLS secret and update the trusted CA configmap.

input.command:
WORKDIR=$(cat /tmp/tls_workdir.txt)
cd "$WORKDIR"
kubectl create secret tls expired-tls-secret -n demo --cert=tls.crt --key=tls.key
kubectl create configmap test-ca -n demo --from-file=ca.crt=ca.crt -o yaml --dry-run=client | kubectl apply -f -

description:

cd "$WORKDIR"
kubectl create secret tls expired-tls-secret -n demo --cert=tls.crt --key=tls.key
kubectl create configmap test-ca -n demo --from-file=ca.crt=ca.crt -o yaml --dry-run=client | kubectl apply -f -

secret/expired-tls-secret created
Warning: resource configmaps/test-ca is missing the kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration annotation which is required by kubectl apply. kubectl apply should only be used on resources created declaratively by either kubectl create --save-config or kubectl apply. The missing annotation will be patched automatically.
configmap/test-ca configured
Shell cwd was reset to /workspace

The missing annotation will be patched automatically.

content.text: Now let's verify the ingress-nginx controller picks up the new secret and test HTTPS access.

input.command:
kubectl get secret -n demo expired-tls-secret
INGRESS_NODE_IP=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}')
INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT=$(kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.port==443)].nodePort}')
echo "NODE_IP=$INGRESS_NODE_IP PORT=$INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT"
sleep 3
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- sh -c \
  'curl -v --cacert /tmp/tls/ca.crt \
    --resolve demo.example.com:'"$INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT"':'"$INGRESS_NODE_IP"' \
    https://demo.example.com:'"$INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT"'/' 2>&1
echo "EXIT: $?"

echo "EXIT: $?"

input.command:
INGRESS_NODE_IP=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}')
INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT=$(kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.port==443)].nodePort}')
kubectl -n demo exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "echo -n | openssl s_client -connect ${INGRESS_NODE_IP}:${INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT} -servername demo.example.com 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -dates"
echo "---curl-test mounted ca.crt---"
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer 2>&1
echo "---configmap current ca.crt subject---"
kubectl get configmap -n demo test-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca crt}' 2>/dev/null
kubectl get configmap -n demo test-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer

input.command:
WORKDIR=$(cat /tmp/tls_workdir.txt)
echo "--- local new ca.crt fingerprint ---"
openssl x509 -in "$WORKDIR/ca.crt" -noout -fingerprint -sha256
echo "--- mounted curl-test ca.crt fingerprint ---"
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt > /tmp/mounted_ca.crt
openssl x509 -in /tmp/mounted_ca.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256
echo "--- configmap ca.crt fingerprint ---"
kubectl get configmap -n demo test-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' > /tmp/configmap_ca.crt
openssl x509 -in /tmp/configmap_ca.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256

input.command: kubectl get pod -n demo curl-test -o jsonpath='{.metadata.ownerReferences}'; echo

for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  fp=$(kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 2>/dev/null)
  echo "attempt $i: $fp"
  if echo "$fp" | grep -q "6E:5E:88:0D"; then
    echo "synced"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done

content.text: Configmap synced to the pod. Now retesting HTTPS access.

input.command:
INGRESS_NODE_IP=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}')
INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT=$(kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx ingress-nginx-controller -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[?(@.port==443)].nodePort}')
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- sh -c \
  'curl -v --cacert /tmp/tls/ca.crt \
    --resolve demo.example.com:'"$INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT"':'"$INGRESS_NODE_IP"' \
    https://demo.example.com:'"$INGRESS_HTTPS_PORT"'/'
echo "EXIT CODE: $?"
